terms.plain_language_definition: Whether Buoy itself is actively keeping the Mac awake.
terms.technical_definition: An immediate On or Off ownership state. On applies Buoy's managed AC pmset profile; Off restores safe saved values, repairs persistent sleep blockers, and verifies the resulting policy.

terms.plain_language_definition: The macOS settings that decide whether automatic and closed-lid sleep are enabled.
terms.technical_definition: The global SleepDisabled value plus the system-sleep timers in the AC and battery pmset profiles. It is reported separately from Buoy mode, display sleep, and temporary assertions.

terms.plain_language_definition: An app or macOS service temporarily delaying idle sleep while it works.
terms.technical_definition: An informational I/O Kit power assertion reported by pmset. It may defer idle sleep but never changes Buoy ownership, persistent policy health, or whether Off succeeded.

terms.plain_language_definition: Updates the selected Buoy settings while mode is already On.
terms.technical_definition: The app action that reapplies the active Buoy configuration. It is unavailable while mode is Off because the mode switch itself performs On and Off transitions immediately.

terms.plain_language_definition: Optional behavior that keeps the Mac awake with the lid closed above a battery floor.
terms.technical_definition: A helper-driven mode that manages SleepDisabled based on power source and battery threshold.

terms.plain_language_definition: How long the screen waits before sleeping.
terms.technical_definition: The independent pmset displaysleep value Buoy applies on AC and restores exactly on Off. A value of 0 keeps the display on but does not prevent system sleep and is not a persistent system-sleep blocker.

terms.plain_language_definition: The battery percentage below which closed-lid awake mode stops staying on.
terms.technical_definition: The clam_min_battery configuration value used by the closed-lid helper.

terms.plain_language_definition: How often Buoy rechecks battery state for closed-lid mode.
terms.technical_definition: The clam_poll_seconds value passed to the closed-lid helper.

terms.plain_language_definition: The saved AC settings Buoy returns to when you turn it off.
terms.technical_definition: The originalValues map stored in ~/.buoy/state.json when mode is applied. System-sleep values are made safe so Off cannot restore sleep=Never; displaysleep is preserved and restored exactly because it does not block system sleep.

terms.plain_language_definition: Buoy's local power-state record.
terms.technical_definition: The JSON file at ~/.buoy/state.json that stores mode state, restore values, config, and helper metadata.

terms.plain_language_definition: The fast storage scan.
terms.technical_definition: StorageScanner summaryOnly mode with bounded per-path work and no fresh largest-file enumeration.

terms.plain_language_definition: The slower, more exact storage scan.
terms.technical_definition: StorageScanner deep mode that walks deeper targets and enumerates large files.

terms.plain_language_definition: Storage data is available, but the current largest-file list is incomplete or missing.
terms.technical_definition: A storage cache or live snapshot where scan mode is summaryOnly and no current deep timestamp is available.

terms.plain_language_definition: Saved storage results for faster tab opening.
terms.technical_definition: The cache record stored at ~/Library/Application Support/Buoy/storage-scan-cache.json.

terms.plain_language_definition: A record of which storage locations Buoy is allowed to scan.
terms.technical_definition: A normalized list of enabled protected scopes and custom paths used to validate cached storage data.

terms.plain_language_definition: A sensitive folder that Buoy scans only after you opt in.
terms.technical_definition: One of the Desktop, Documents, Downloads, or Pictures scopes managed by StorageAccessManager.

terms.plain_language_definition: The saved permission Buoy uses to revisit a folder you approved.
terms.technical_definition: Bookmark data stored in UserDefaults and resolved into a security-scoped URL at scan time.

terms.plain_language_definition: Buoy's advice about how safe an item might be to review for cleanup.
terms.technical_definition: The StorageSafety classification: Likely Safe, Review First, or Essential.

terms.plain_language_definition: Space Buoy thinks is worth reviewing for cleanup.
terms.technical_definition: The sum of storage items currently classified as cleanup candidates.

terms.plain_language_definition: macOS's current thermal stress level for the machine.
terms.technical_definition: The ProcessInfo thermalState rendered as Nominal, Fair, Serious, or Critical.

terms.plain_language_definition: The top summary that tells you whether the machine looks ready, busy, tight, or under thermal watch.
terms.technical_definition: The derived summary in OverviewViewController based on thermal, CPU, memory, disk, and battery heuristics.
